mappings.reason: Configuring data access control lists directly enforces access limitations on data assets like cryptographic keystores, satisfying the technical access control requirement.
Defining and maintaining role-based access control establishes the authorization rights that determine which personnel are authorized to access keystores.
Following an access granting process ensures that only authorized personnel are provisioned with access, supporting the limitation requirement.
Following an access revoking process ensures that access is removed when personnel are no longer authorized, maintaining the access limitation.
Restricting administrator privileges to dedicated accounts limits the pool of personnel with high-level access often required for keystore management.
Managing default accounts prevents unauthorized access through pre-configured vendor accounts that may have keystore permissions.
